title: Limit Management of Audit Logging to a Subset of Privileged Users
description: Limit management of audit logging to a subset of privileged users.

checks.name: Manage Auditing and Security Log Right is Restricted
checks.purpose: Verify the 'Manage auditing and security log' user right (SeSecurityPrivilege) is assigned only to Administrators and not to standard users

checks.name: Audit Policy Cannot Be Modified by Standard Users
checks.purpose: Confirm standard user accounts do not have rights to modify audit policy via auditpol or Group Policy

checks.name: Manage Auditing and Security Log Right is Restricted
checks.purpose: Verify the 'Manage auditing and security log' user right (SeSecurityPrivilege) is assigned only to Administrators or a dedicated audit management group

checks.name: Audit Policy Cannot Be Modified by Standard Users
checks.purpose: Confirm standard user accounts do not have rights to modify audit policy via auditpol or Group Policy

checks.name: Event Log Readers Group Membership is Controlled
checks.purpose: Verify the Event Log Readers group contains only authorized accounts and does not include standard users or unauthorized service accounts

checks.name: SIEM Access is Restricted to Authorized Personnel
checks.purpose: Confirm access to the centralized SIEM or log management platform is limited to authorized security and audit personnel

checks.name: Only Root Can Manage Auditd
checks.purpose: Verify that auditctl, auditd service management, and audit configuration files in /etc/audit/ are accessible only by root

checks.name: Sudo Rights for Audit Management are Restricted
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/sudoers does not grant unrestricted sudo for auditctl or auditd commands to non-administrative accounts

checks.name: Audit Log Directory is Not Accessible to Standard Users
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/ directory permissions are 700 (root only) so standard users cannot read, modify, or delete audit logs

checks.name: Only Root Can Manage Auditd
checks.purpose: Verify that auditctl, auditd service management, and audit configuration files in /etc/audit/ are accessible only by root

checks.name: Sudo Rights for Audit Management are Restricted
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/sudoers does not grant unrestricted sudo for auditctl or auditd commands to non-administrative accounts

checks.name: Audit Log Directory is Not Accessible to Standard Users
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/ directory permissions are 700 (root only) so standard users cannot read, modify, or delete audit logs

remediation: Restrict SeSecurityPrivilege to Administrators only via Group Policy. Control Event Log Readers group membership. On Linux, ensure /etc/audit/ and /var/log/audit/ are root-only. Review sudoers for unrestricted audit tool access. Restrict SIEM access to authorized security personnel only.
